stem: A factory produces aircraft rivets using two sources. Forge A handles 79% of production with a known defect rate of 4.4%. Forge B handles 21% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 5.7% and 10.0%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected aircraft rivet is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from Forge B?

ground_truth.answer: P(Forge B | defective) ranges from 0.256 to 0.377 as Forge B's defect rate varies from 5.7% to 10.0%. Point estimate at 7.9%: 0.323.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.256, 0.377] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
